steps.title: Read circle P from standard form
steps.content: Use $(x-h)^2+(y-k)^2=r^2$ to read circle P's center and radius, then apply the stated shift and diameter change to circle Q.

steps.title: Identify center and radius of circle P
steps.content: Standard form $(x-h)^2 + (y-k)^2 = r^2$ has center $(h,k)$ and radius $r$. For $(x-3)^2 + (y+7)^2 = 25$: center $(3, -7)$, radius $\sqrt{25} = 5$.

steps.title: Find radius of circle Q
steps.content: Diameter of Q is double that of P. P has diameter $10$, so Q has diameter $20$ and radius $10$. Thus $r^2 = 100$.

steps.title: Write the final equation
steps.content: Circle Q has center $(4,-9)$ and radius $10$, so its equation is $$(x-4)^2+(y+9)^2=100.$$ That matches D.
